steps.title: Identify the prediction and what would contradict it
steps.content: The student predicts that birds with body masses over 150 grams do not use deceptive defenses. To weaken this claim, we need evidence of a bird exceeding 150 grams that actually does use broken-wing display—a direct counterexample.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B states that the pied-billed grebe weighs 409 grams and performs broken-wing display.

At 409 grams, this bird far exceeds the 150-gram threshold while actively using a deceptive defense, directly contradicting the student's prediction.

The other choices either lack sufficient mass data to establish a counterexample (A and C) or describe birds under 150 grams, which are consistent with rather than contradictory to the prediction (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
